implications: Applicant CV: Lists extensive research roles (Research Fellow, Advisor) and grants (NIHR, ESRC) demonstrating relevant experience and training in dementia and health services research.
Applicant Research Background: Details 10 years of experience across all research stages, including RCTs, qualitative methods, and systematic reviews, demonstrating relevant experience and training.
Applicant CV: Provides a comprehensive publication record (20+ papers) in peer-reviewed journals and lists multiple grants and awards, indicating strong research outputs.
Applicant Research Background: Lists 33 publications, h-index 13, and specific grants (NIHR, ESRC), providing strong evidence of impactful research outputs and outcomes.

Applicant CV: Shows progression from PhD candidate to Research Fellow and Lead Applicant on grants, plus participation in leadership programs (Springboard, GW4 Crucible), indicating a trajectory towards research leadersh

Applicant Research Background: Shows increasing responsibility through leading trials (INFORM), securing funding, and serving as an Early Career Representative, indicating a trajectory towards research leadership.
Applicant CV: Demonstrates involvement in practice-based research through roles at NIHR Research Design Service and Dementia Centre, aligning with practitioner-academic career criteria.
Applicant Research Background: Describes work in adult social care research and co-production with unpaid carers, demonstrating commitment to a practitioner-academic career.
Applicant CV: Evidence of prior successful projects (NIHR, ESRC grants) and a robust publication record supports the quality of previous research.
Applicant Research Background: Provides detailed evidence of prior successful projects (INFORM, MOVE) and a robust publication record, supporting the quality of previous research.
Applicant Research Background: Clearly articulates a research vision focused on unpaid carers' wellbeing and a career trajectory towards leadership in dementia research.
Applicant Research Background: States the Fellowship is a 'crucial step' to establish leadership and generate co-produced knowledge, explaining why this award is the right next step.
Applicant Research Background: Describes co-producing the Fellowship application with a team of eight unpaid carers, demonstrating integration of lived experience in research design.
Plain English Summary of Research: Confirms unpaid carers co-produced the application and will act as co-researchers, reinforcing wp.1.
Scientific Abstract: Confirms eight unpaid carers co-produced the research and will be part of the core team, supporting wp.1.
Detailed Research Plan: Confirms co-production with eight unpaid carers who identified research concerns and will participate in Delphi exercises and focus groups, supporting wp.1.

Patient & Public Involvement: Unpaid carers co-produced the application, identified goals, and provided feedback on methods and dissemination, demonstrating deep integration of lived experience in research design.
Applicant Research Background: Mentions dissemination to diverse audiences including carers' organisations, blogs, and podcasts, showing commitment to maximising public benefit.
Plain English Summary of Research: Details dissemination via podcasts, articles, and illustrators to reach carers and professionals, reinforcing wp.3.
Scientific Abstract: Details dissemination via podcasts, webinars, and articles to reach carers and professionals, supporting wp.3.
Detailed Research Plan: Describes a robust dissemination strategy including a documentary, blogs, and engagement with policy makers to maximize public benefit, supporting wp.3.
Plain English Summary of Research: Clearly outlines aims, background, methods, and dissemination in accessible language, supporting pr.1.
Scientific Abstract: Clearly outlines research question, background, aims, methods, and dissemination in accessible language, supporting pr.1.

Scientific Abstract: Describes a feasible three-stage design with specific timelines and sample sizes (100 carers, 30 consensus), supporting pr.3.
